triggers: devkit ship of a UI-affecting diff in a repo with .qavis/recipe.json
PR does not exist yet at commit time so existingBody has no qavis marker

edgeCases.scenario: devkit ship a branch with one .tsx under components/ and a .qavis/recipe.json present; qavis has NOT been run
edgeCases.expected: ship should be satisfiable by ACTUALLY running qavis (a pass-receipt clears the block), not only by an override
edgeCases.notes: As specced, hasQavisMarker(existingBody)=false on every new ship -> ADVISE -> exit 3 ALWAYS. v1 defers the pass-receipt, so running qavis cannot clear it; only an override can. Pure-friction always-block on the flagship use case.

edgeCases.scenario: same UI ship, second attempt after running `qavis qa --diff` locally
edgeCases.expected: block clears because qavis ran and passed on this staged tree
edgeCases.notes: No staged-diff-SHA pass-receipt in v1 -> the second attempt STILL blocks. Trains reflexive override -> dead gate (the exact anti-pattern check-sentry.mts lines 14-16 warn against).

triggers: commit trailer `qavis: skip` used to override a PRE-COMMIT gate

edgeCases.scenario: author adds trailer `qavis: skip` to the commit message to bypass the advisory at pre-commit
edgeCases.notes: A pre-commit hook has NO access to the commit message (git passes it only to commit-msg). Sentry & completeness live at commit-msg specifically for this. The trailer override is DEAD at pre-commit; only env overrides work.

triggers: bench `diff` field vs gate `git diff --cached` feeding the shared classifier

edgeCases.scenario: consumer has diff.noprefix=true or mnemonicPrefix set; gate calls plain `git diff --cached`
edgeCases.expected: judge sees identical header shape to authored bench diffs (no drift)
edgeCases.notes: completeness/detect force `-c diff.noprefix=false -c diff.mnemonicPrefix=false`. Plan omits this -> consumer git config can change the bytes the shared buildContext sees, drifting gate from bench.

triggers: override env var propagation through the ship subprocess chain

edgeCases.scenario: user runs `GUARD_QAVIS_OK=1 devkit ship` (inline prefix) under an rtk command-rewriting shell hook
edgeCases.expected: override reaches the git-commit subprocess inside commit-with-gate-capture.sh
edgeCases.notes: Memory + the ship script warn inline env prefixes can be stripped by command-rewriting hooks; must be EXPORTED. Document `export GUARD_QAVIS_OK=1` (same footgun as SHIP_COMMIT_TIMEOUT).

triggers: LLM/hybrid judge outage during a strict ship

edgeCases.scenario: claude CLI unauth/quota-exhausted while gate runs in llm mode under GUARD_AI_STRICT
edgeCases.expected: exit 2 fail-open (advisor infra failure must not block a ship)
edgeCases.notes: Intended departure from completeness (which exit-3 fail-closes on outage). Fine IF the husky fragment does NOT render the standard exit-3 'judge unavailable' remedy for this gate. Regex mode never hits this.

triggers: the actionable command the gate prints

edgeCases.scenario: gate prints `qavis qa --diff <base> --repo . --annotate description` (no --pr) as the remedy
edgeCases.expected: the printed command actually records something the author can act on
edgeCases.notes: qavis --annotate only writes on a PASS and requires --pr (cli.ts printAnnotation returns early when pr undefined). At commit time there is no PR, so `--annotate description` is a silent no-op. The remedy command is broken for the primary case.

edgeCases.scenario: UI change in a repo with NO .qavis/recipe.json
edgeCases.expected: SILENT (correct: can't QA without a recipe; don't nag every commit)
edgeCases.notes: Plan's SILENT is right for the gate. Surface 'add a recipe' ONCE in devkit doctor/init, not per-commit.

edgeCases.scenario: positive ADVISE returns exit 3 under strict; husky GUARD_FRAGMENTS.qavisAdvisory reuses decisions/review rc handling
edgeCases.expected: the message tells the author to run qavis, NOT 'judge unavailable — check claude auth/quota'
edgeCases.notes: husky-block.mts decisions/review fragments render exit 3 as 'judge unavailable — strict ship mode failed closed'. Repurposing exit 3 for a positive finding collides with that copy; the fragment CANNOT be 'same as decisions/review'.

edgeCases.scenario: mirror sentry bench `--fail` (aggregate F1/accuracy delta with 1e-9 epsilon)
edgeCases.expected: gate on per-row FLIP TABLE (McNemar) + hard floors + config/corpus/gate hashes, per the recorded Target
edgeCases.notes: sentry/eval/bench.mts line 135 IS the aggregate-delta anti-pattern docs/decisions/bench-gates-on-flips-not-deltas.md was written to replace. Use gate-engine/review/eval/bench.mts as the --fail template.

edgeCases.scenario: mine devkit/qavis PR history for ADVISE-labelled rows
edgeCases.expected: labels reflect 'should this have been QA'd', independently judged
edgeCases.notes: Label circularity if ADVISE == 'qavis was historically run'. Also devkit has frontendRoots:[] (a CLI, zero UI diffs) -> almost nothing UI to mine from devkit; corpus is really authored + qavis-only.

edgeCases.scenario: staged change is a pure DELETE of components/Foo.tsx
edgeCases.expected: decide explicitly (likely SILENT — nothing to drive on a removed view)
edgeCases.notes: isUiAffecting matches the deleted .tsx path -> regex ADVISE. False-advise on deletions unless name-status filters D.

edgeCases.scenario: staged change is a binary asset under public/ or assets/
edgeCases.expected: ADVISE (visual change), and the diff-reading judge must not flip to SILENT on an empty/binary hunk
edgeCases.notes: regex ADVISE is right; the LLM diff path gets no usable text for a binary hunk -> ensure it does not downgrade.
completeness returns 0 on files.length===0; qavis-advisory must too.

edgeCases.scenario: monorepo: UI change in package A which HAS a recipe; hook runs cd'd into A
edgeCases.expected: recipe resolves per-package (.qavis/recipe.json relative to the package cwd)
edgeCases.notes: husky-block cd's into pkgRel; recipe presence must resolve at that cwd, not the git root. Per synced-assets-layout-agnostic, resolve roots from config, never hardcode.

edgeCases.expected: classifier handles rename entries and extension changes deterministically
edgeCases.notes: git diff --cached --name-only lists the new path; a .tsx->.ts rename could drop below UI_EXT. Define the intended verdict.
